title: mentha × maximilianea var. schultzii
intro: Mentha × maximilianea var. schultzii, also known as bergamot mint, is a hybrid species of mint that is a cross between Mentha aquatica and Mentha citrata. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of essential oils, which contain compounds like linalool and menthone that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of bergamot mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to alleviate stress and improve mood. Bergamot mint can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
